question: [File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: UtilsLib.mulDivDown / TakeAmountsLib.buyerAssetsToUnits] Can an unprivileged taker using MidnightBundles.buyWithAssetsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral with a sell offer (offer.buy=false) trigger a rounding mismatch where periphery computes units=mulDivDown(targetBuyerAssets,WAD,buyerPrice) but core computes buyerAssets=mulDivUp(units,buyerPrice,WAD), causing filledBuyerAssets to exceed targetFilledBuyerAssets by 1 wei, violating the invariant that require(filledBuyerAssets==targetFilledBuyerAssets) must hold, causing scoped impact: user transaction reverts with OutOfOffers even though sufficient offers exist and user has paid gas? Proof idea: fuzz targetBuyerAssets and buyerPrice over all tick prices, assert mulDivUp(mulDivDown(target,WAD,price),price,WAD)==target for all inputs.

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: UtilsLib.mulDivUp / TakeAmountsLib.sellerAssetsToUnits] Can an unprivileged taker using MidnightBundles.supplyCollateralAndSellWithAssetsTarget with a buy offer (offer.buy=true) trigger a rounding mismatch where periphery computes units=mulDivUp(targetSellerAssets,WAD,sellerPrice) but core computes sellerAssets=mulDivDown(units,sellerPrice,WAD), causing filledSellerAssets to be less than targetFilledSellerAssets, violating the invariant that require(filledSellerAssets==targetFilledSellerAssets) must hold, causing scoped impact: user transaction reverts with OutOfOffers even though sufficient offers exist? Proof idea: fuzz targetSellerAssets and sellerPrice, assert mulDivDown(mulDivUp(target,WAD,price),price,WAD)==target for all tick-valid prices.

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: UtilsLib.mulDivDown / ConsumableUnitsLib.consumableUnits] Can an unprivileged taker trigger a scenario where ConsumableUnitsLib.consumableUnits for an assets-based sell offer returns units=mulDivDown(remaining,WAD,sellerPrice), but core computes sellerAssets=mulDivUp(units,sellerPrice,WAD) which exceeds offer.maxAssets-consumed, causing core to revert with ConsumedAssets, the bundler silently catches and skips the offer, and the user receives OutOfOffers even though the offer had nonzero remaining capacity? Proof idea: unit test with sellerPrice such that mulDivUp(mulDivDown(remaining,WAD,sellerPrice),sellerPrice,WAD)>remaining.

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: UtilsLib.mulDivUp / ConsumableUnitsLib.consumableUnits] Can an unprivileged taker trigger a scenario where ConsumableUnitsLib.consumableUnits for an assets-based buy offer returns units=mulDivUp(remaining,WAD,buyerPrice), but core computes buyerAssets=mulDivDown(units,buyerPrice,WAD) which is less than remaining, causing the offer to appear not fully consumed, and the bundler to compute unitsToTake larger than what core will accept without exceeding maxAssets, causing core to revert with ConsumedAssets and the bundler to silently skip the offer? Proof idea: fuzz remaining and buyerPrice, assert mulDivDown(mulDivUp(remaining,WAD,price),price,WAD)>=remaining.

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: UtilsLib.zeroFloorSub / TakeAmountsLib.buyerAssetsToUnits] Can an unprivileged taker cause TakeAmountsLib.buyerAssetsToUnits to compute units using a stale settlement fee because zeroFloorSub(offer.market.maturity,block.timestamp) is evaluated at view time while the actual take executes one block later with a different block.timestamp, causing the units computed by periphery to yield a different buyerAssets in core than targetBuyerAssets, violating the exact-equality invariant in buyWithAssetsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral? Proof idea: differential test between settlementFee at t and t+1 near fee breakpoints (1d,7d,30d,90d,180d,360d).

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: UtilsLib.zeroFloorSub / TakeAmountsLib.sellerAssetsToUnits] Can an unprivileged taker cause TakeAmountsLib.sellerAssetsToUnits to compute units using a stale settlement fee because zeroFloorSub(offer.market.maturity,block.timestamp) is evaluated at view time while the actual take executes at a different timestamp, causing the units computed by periphery to yield a different sellerAssets in core than targetSellerAssets, violating the exact-equality invariant in supplyCollateralAndSellWithAssetsTarget? Proof idea: fuzz block.timestamp delta between view and execution near maturity boundary.

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: UtilsLib.zeroFloorSub / ConsumableUnitsLib.consumableUnits] Can an unprivileged taker cause ConsumableUnitsLib.consumableUnits to return 0 for an assets-based offer where consumed[maker][group] > offer.maxAssets (possible after a group-shared offer is over-consumed via a different offer in the same group), because zeroFloorSub(offer.maxAssets,consumed)=0 causes buyerAssetsToUnits(midnight,id,offer,0)=0, causing min(remaining,takes[i].units,0)=0, causing the bundler to silently skip the offer and eventually revert with OutOfOffers? Proof idea: set up two offers sharing a group, fill the first to exhaust the group budget, then call bundler with the second offer.

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: UtilsLib.mulDivDown / MidnightBundles.buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral referral fee] Can an unprivileged taker trigger an arithmetic underflow in the refund computation maxBuyerAssets-filledBuyerAssets-referralFeeAssets where referralFeeAssets=filledBuyerAssets.mulDivDown(referralFeePct,WAD-referralFeePct) rounds down, but filledBuyerAssets+referralFeeAssets still exceeds maxBuyerAssets due to the asymmetric fee formula, causing the transaction to revert and the user to lose gas after tokens were already pulled? Proof idea: fuzz referralFeePct near WAD-1 and filledBuyerAssets near maxBuyerAssets, assert no underflow.

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: UtilsLib.mulDivDown / MidnightBundles.supplyCollateralAndSellWithAssetsTarget referral fee] Can an unprivileged taker trigger a scenario where referralFeeAssets=targetSellerAssets.mulDivDown(referralFeePct,WAD-referralFeePct) and targetFilledSellerAssets=targetSellerAssets+referralFeeAssets, but the actual filledSellerAssets from core differs from targetFilledSellerAssets by 1 wei due to rounding in sellerAssetsToUnits, causing require(filledSellerAssets==targetFilledSellerAssets) to revert and the user to lose gas after collateral was already supplied? Proof idea: unit test with targetSellerAssets=1 and referralFeePct=WAD/2.

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: UtilsLib.mulDivDown / MidnightBundles.supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget referral fee] Can an unprivileged taker trigger a scenario where referralFeeAssets=filledSellerAssets.mulDivDown(referralFeePct,WAD) rounds down such that filledSellerAssets-referralFeeAssets barely fails require(filledSellerAssets-referralFeeAssets>=minSellerAssets) even though the user expected to receive minSellerAssets, causing the transaction to revert with SellerAssetsTooLow and the user to lose gas after collateral was already supplied? [File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: UtilsLib.min / MidnightBundles.min three-way] Can an unprivileged taker trigger a scenario where min(targetUnits-filledUnits, takes[i].units, consumableUnits) returns a value that is larger than what core will accept without exceeding offer.maxAssets, because consumableUnits uses mulDivUp rounding that overestimates remaining capacity, causing core to revert with ConsumedAssets, the bundler to silently skip the offer, and the user to receive OutOfOffers? [File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: UtilsLib.mulDivDown / TakeAmountsLib.buyerAssetsToUnits with buyerPrice=WAD] Can an unprivileged taker trigger a rounding edge case where buyerPrice=WAD (tick=0, price=1e18) causes mulDivDown(targetBuyerAssets,WAD,WAD)=targetBuyerAssets and mulDivUp(targetBuyerAssets,WAD,WAD)=targetBuyerAssets, making the round-trip exact, but a settlement fee of 0 causes sellerPrice=buyerPrice=WAD and the require(buyerPrice<=WAD) check in buyerAssetsToUnits to pass at the boundary, while a 1-wei settlement fee increase causes buyerPrice=WAD+1 and revert, causing user DoS? [File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: UtilsLib.zeroFloorSub / TakeAmountsLib post-maturity TTM=0] Can an unprivileged taker cause TakeAmountsLib.buyerAssetsToUnits to compute units using settlementFee(id,0) (the 0d breakpoint fee) when block.timestamp==offer.market.maturity because zeroFloorSub(maturity,maturity)=0, while core also uses zeroFloorSub(maturity,block.timestamp)=0 and the same 0d fee, but a 1-second difference between periphery view and core execution causes periphery to use TTM=0 while core uses TTM=1, yielding different settlement fees and a 1-wei buyerAssets discrepancy? [File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: UtilsLib.mulDivDown / TakeAmountsLib.buyerAssetsToUnits with multiple offers] Can an unprivileged taker trigger a scenario where buyWithAssetsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral fills multiple offers and the cumulative rounding error from mulDivDown across N offers causes filledBuyerAssets to deviate from targetFilledBuyerAssets by up to N wei, causing the equality check to fail even though each individual offer was filled correctly?
